column1: -- Transaction latency in NEAR with a full percentile distribution (p50
-- Step 1: Get the starting information for each transaction. (No changes here)
-- Step 2: Find the timestamp of the FINAL execution outcome for each transaction. (No changes here)
-- Step 3: Join and calculate a wider range of latency statistics.
-- The APPROX_QUANTILES function returns an array of values for each percentile.
-- We select the specific percentile we want using [OFFSET(...)].
-- p50 (Median): The "typical" experience. 50% of txs were faster than this.
-- p80: 80% of txs were faster than this value.
-- p90: 90% of txs were faster than this value.
-- p95: The "reasonably worst-case" experience for the vast majority.
-- p99: The experience of all but the absolute slowest 1% of transactions.
